pet.name: Biscuit
pet.special_needs:
Large senior dog with weight management needs. Needs moderate
  exercise, joint support, slower-paced walks, and feeding consistency.
pet.characteristics: Large senior dogs typically require moderate, low-impact exercise such as slow-paced walks to protect aging joints; consistent feeding schedules with portion control and joint-supportive nutrition help manage weight and mobility. Monitor for arthritis, dental health, and cognitive changes. Provide orthopedic bedding, easy access to food and water, and a calm environment. Regular veterinary check-ups become increasingly important for early detection of age-related conditions. Mental enrichment through gentle play and routine maintains quality of life.
pet.species_profile_summary: Large senior dogs commonly live 8-12 years in captive care; this individual benefits from weight management, joint support, and consistent low-stress routines.

age_context.summary: Large senior dogs commonly live 8-12 years in captive care; this individual benefits from weight management, joint support, and consistent low-stress routines.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Senior pets often need lower-impact exercise, more frequent comfort checks, and closer observation of appetite, hydration, and mobility. Shorter walks, joint-friendly movement, and calm enrichment are often easier to tolerate than intense activity.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

retrieved_passages.title: Medication Routine and Safety
retrieved_passages.content: Medication-related tasks in a home routine should focus on reminders, documentation, and observation, not dosage changes. A planning assistant can remind the owner to give medication exactly as already prescribed and to log whether the dose was completed.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning feeding with hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Feed Biscuit according to veterinary prescribed plan. Ensure fresh water is accessible. Monitor appetite and water intake as part of daily observation.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets require consistent feeding schedules with portion control for weight management. Daily hydration monitoring is essential for aging dogs.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening feeding with hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Feed Biscuit according to veterinary prescribed plan. Ensure fresh water is accessible. Log any changes in appetite or behavior.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent feeding schedules support weight management and provide routine comfort for senior dogs. Evening feeding allows monitoring before rest period.

raw_recommendations.name: Gentle indoor exercise or sniff walk
raw_recommendations.notes: Due to rainy weather, conduct indoor sniff walks, slow-paced movement around home, or gentle play. Avoid high-impact activity to protect aging joints. Adjust based on Biscuit's mobility and energy level.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior dogs need lower-impact exercise to support joint health. Indoor alternatives during rain provide necessary movement and mental enrichment without weather exposure.

raw_recommendations.name: Afternoon comfort check and calm enrichment
raw_recommendations.notes: Spend time with Biscuit doing calm interaction such as gentle brushing, quiet play, or companionship. Observe mobility, comfort level, and any signs of discomfort or behavioral changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Daily observation of appetite, hydration, and mobility is critical for senior pets. Calm enrichment improves quality of life and allows early detection of changes.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening mobility and comfort assessment
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe Biscuit's movement, gait, and comfort level. Note any stiffness, limping, or difficulty rising. Ensure orthopedic bedding is accessible and comfortable for rest.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior dogs require frequent comfort checks and closer observation of mobility. Evening assessment helps identify joint issues or pain before overnight rest.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly gentle grooming and skin check
raw_recommendations.notes: Brush Biscuit gently, check skin condition, ears, and paws. This supports circulation, allows monitoring for lumps or skin issues, and provides bonding time.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Regular grooming and skin checks are part of comprehensive senior pet care. Gentle brushing improves quality of life and allows early detection of health changes.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly veterinary check-in reminder
raw_recommendations.notes: Schedule or confirm monthly veterinary check-ups. Regular check-ups are increasingly important for early detection of age-related conditions in senior dogs.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets benefit from regular veterinary oversight. Monthly check-ups support early detection of arthritis, dental issues, and other age-related conditions.

raw_recommendations.name: Weight and body condition monitoring
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe Biscuit's body condition weekly. Track any weight changes. Follow veterinary prescribed feeding plan without adjusting portions independently.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Weight management is essential for senior dogs with joint support needs. Consistent monitoring helps ensure feeding plan effectiveness without requiring dietary changes.
